always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin
always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trst_ni) begin
always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trst_ni) begin

reset_facts.text_preview:
input  logic                  clk_i,                      // Clock
    input  logic                  rst_ni,                     // Asynchronous reset active low
    input  logic                  time_irq_i,                 // Timer threw a interrupt
    // send a flush request out if a CSR with a side effect has changed (e.g. written)
    output logic                  flush_o,
    output logic                  halt_csr_o,                 // halt requested

reset_facts.signal_or_condition: always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin

reset_facts.text_preview:
input  logic                              clk_i,              // Clock
    input  logic                              rst_ni,             // Asynchronous reset active low
    input  logic                              testmode_i,
    input  logic                              dmi_rst_ni,         // Debug Module Interface reset, active-low
    input  logic                              dmi_req_valid_i,
    output logic                              dmi_req_ready_o,

input  logic                              testmode_i,
    input  logic                              dmi_rst_ni,         // Debug Module Interface reset, active-low
    input  logic                              dmi_req_valid_i,
    output logic                              dmi_req_ready_o,
    input  dm::dmi_req_t                      dmi_req_i,
    // every request needs a response one cycle later

.clk_i            ( clk_i                ),
        .rst_ni           ( dmi_rst_ni           ), // reset only when system is re-set
        .flush_i          ( 1'b0                 ), // we do not need to flush this queue
        .testmode_i       ( testmode_i           ),
        .full_o           ( resp_queue_full      ),
        .empty_o          ( resp_queue_empty     ),

reset_facts.signal_or_condition: always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin
@(posedge clk_i) disable iff (~rst_ni) (dmi_req_ready_o && dmi_req_valid_i && dtm_op == dm::DTM_READ) |->

reset_facts.text_preview:
input  logic               clk_i,       // clock
    input  logic               rst_ni,      // asynchronous reset active low, connect PoR here, not the system reset
    input  logic               testmode_i,
    output logic               ndmreset_o,  // non-debug module reset
    output logic               dmactive_o,  // debug module is active
    output logic [NrHarts-1:0] debug_req_o, // async debug request

input  logic               testmode_i,
    output logic               ndmreset_o,  // non-debug module reset
    output logic               dmactive_o,  // debug module is active
    output logic [NrHarts-1:0] debug_req_o, // async debug request
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0] unavailable_i, // communicate whether the hart is unavailable (e.g.: power down)

// Connection to DTM - compatible to RocketChip Debug Module
    input  logic               dmi_rst_ni,
    input  logic               dmi_req_valid_i,
    output logic               dmi_req_ready_o,
    input  dm::dmi_req_t       dmi_req_i,

.clk_i                   ( clk_i                 ),
        .rst_ni                  ( rst_ni                ),
        .testmode_i              ( testmode_i            ),
        .dmi_rst_ni,
        .dmi_req_valid_i,
        .dmi_req_ready_o,

        .dmi_req_valid_i,
        .dmi_req_ready_o,
        .dmi_req_i,
        .dmi_resp_valid_o,

.dmi_resp_o,

        .dmactive_o              ( dmactive_o            ),
        .hartsel_o               ( hartsel               ),

.clk_i                   ( clk_i                 ),

input  logic         clk_i,      // DMI Clock
    input  logic         rst_ni,     // Asynchronous reset active low
    input  logic         testmode_i,
    input  logic [31:0]  jtag_key, // key for jtag

    output logic         dmi_rst_no, // hard reset

output logic         dmi_rst_no, // hard reset
    output dm::dmi_req_t dmi_req_o,
    output logic         dmi_req_valid_o,
    input  logic         dmi_req_ready_i,

input  logic         tms_i,    // JTAG test mode select pad
    input  logic         trst_ni,  // JTAG test reset pad
    input  logic         td_i,     // JTAG test data input pad
    output logic         td_o,     // JTAG test data output pad
    output logic         tdo_oe_o, // Data out output enable
    output logic         umode_o   // Sets the processor to machine mode

if (test_logic_reset) begin
            dr_d = '0;
        end
    end

reset_facts.signal_or_condition: always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trst_ni) begin

reset_facts.text_preview:
always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
        if (~rst_ni) begin
            pass <= jtag_key;
        end 
    end

.tms_i,
        .trst_ni,
        .td_i,
        .td_o,
        .tdo_oe_o,

.testmode_i         ( testmode_i       ),

        .shift_dr_o         ( shift_dr         ),
        .update_dr_o        ( update_dr        ),
        .capture_dr_o       ( capture_dr       ),
        .dmi_access_o       ( dmi_access       ),

.tck_i,
        .trst_ni,
        .jtag_dmi_req_i    ( dmi_req          ),

        .jtag_dmi_resp_o   ( dmi_resp         ),

.clk_i,
        .rst_ni,
        .core_dmi_req_o    ( dmi_req_o        ),
        .core_dmi_valid_o  ( dmi_req_valid_o  ),
        .core_dmi_ready_i  ( dmi_req_ready_i  ),
        .core_dmi_resp_i   ( dmi_resp_i       ),

input  logic        tms_i,    // JTAG test mode select pad
    input  logic        trst_ni,  // JTAG test reset pad
    input  logic        td_i,     // JTAG test data input pad
    output logic        td_o,     // JTAG test data output pad
    output logic        tdo_oe_o, // Data out output enable
    input  logic        testmode_i,

input  logic        testmode_i,
    output logic        test_logic_reset_o,
    output logic        shift_dr_o,
    output logic        update_dr_o,
    output logic        capture_dr_o,

enum logic [3:0] { TestLogicReset, RunTestIdle, SelectDrScan,
                     CaptureDr, ShiftDr, Exit1Dr, PauseDr, Exit2Dr,
                     UpdateDr, SelectIrScan, CaptureIr, ShiftIr,
                     Exit1Ir, PauseIr, Exit2Ir, UpdateIr } tap_state_q, tap_state_d;

zero1        : '0,
                                dmihardreset : 1'b0,
                                dmireset     : 1'b0,
                                zero0        : '0,
                                idle         : 'd1,         // 1: Enter Run-Test/Idle and leave it immediately
                                dmistat      : dmi_error_i, // 0: No error, 1: Op failed, 2: too fast

dmihardreset : 1'b0,
                                dmireset     : 1'b0,
                                zero0        : '0,
                                idle         : 'd1,         // 1: Enter Run-Test/Idle and leave it immediately
                                dmistat      : dmi_error_i, // 0: No error, 1: Op failed, 2: too fast
                                abits        : 'd7, // The size of address in dmi

end
            default: tap_state_d = TestLogicReset;  // can't actually happen
      endcase
    end

    always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trst_ni) begin

        .req_o  ( rom_req                 ),
        .we_o   ( rom_we                  ),
        .addr_o ( rom_addr                ),

.clk_i      ( clk_i                             ),
        .rst_ni     ( ndmreset_n                        ),
        .aw_valid_i ( master[ariane_soc::DRAM].aw_valid ),
        .aw_chan_i  ( aw_chan_i                         ),
        .aw_ready_o ( master[ariane_soc::DRAM].aw_ready ),
        .w_valid_i  ( master[ariane_soc::DRAM].w_valid  ),

.clk_i  ( clk_i      ),
        .rst_ni ( ndmreset_n ),
        .slave  ( dram       ),
        .req_o  ( req        ),
        .we_o   ( we         ),
        .addr_o ( addr       ),

.clk_i      ( clk_i                                                                       ),
        .rst_ni     ( rst_ni                                                                      ),
        .req_i      ( req                                                                         ),
        .we_i       ( we                                                                          ),
        .addr_i     ( addr[$clog2(NUM_WORDS)-1+$clog2(AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8):$clog2(AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8)] ),
        .wdata_i    ( wdata                                                                       ),

.clk          ( clk_i      ),
        .rst_n        ( ndmreset_n ),
        .test_en_i    ( test_en    ),
        .slave        ( slave      ),
        .master       ( master     ),
	.priv_lvl_i   ( priv_lvl   ),

.clk_i       ( clk_i          ),
        .rst_ni      ( ndmreset_n     ),
        .testmode_i  ( test_en        ),
        .axi_req_i   ( axi_clint_req  ),
        .axi_resp_o  ( axi_clint_resp ),
        .rtc_i       ( rtc_i          ),

.clk_i             ( clk_i                        ),
      .rst_ni            ( ndmreset_n                   ),
      .plic              ( master[ariane_soc::PLIC]     ),
      .uart              ( master[ariane_soc::UART]     ),
      .aes               ( master[ariane_soc::AES]      ), 
      .rom2_fuse         ( master[ariane_soc::ROM2]     ),

.eth_rxd           ( ),
      .eth_rst_n         ( ),
      .eth_tx_en         ( ),
      .eth_txd           ( ),
      .phy_mdio          ( ),
      .eth_mdc           ( ),

.clk_i                ( clk_i               ),
        .rst_ni               ( ndmreset_n          ),
        .boot_addr_i          ( ariane_soc::ROMBase ), // start fetching from ROM
        .hart_id_i            ( '0                  ),
        .irq_i                ( irqs                ),
        .ipi_i                ( ipi                 ),

csr_facts.text: end else if (!debug_mode_q && csr_op_i == WFI && !ex_i.valid) begin
* Description: Debug CSRs. Communication over Debug Transport Module (DTM)
@(posedge clk_i) disable iff (~rst_ni) (dmi_req_ready_o && dmi_req_valid_i && dtm_op == dm::DTM_READ) |->
* Description: Top-level of debug module (DM). This is an AXI-Slave.
*              DTM protocol is equal to SiFives debug protocol to leverage
enum logic [2:0] { Idle, Read, WaitReadValid, Write, WaitWriteValid } state_d, state_q;
* Description: JTAG TAP for DMI (according to debug spec 0.13)
* ROM2: Which have all the keys.

asset_candidates.name: CSR registers
asset_candidates.description: RISC-V control and status registers controlling exception handling, privilege delegation, address translation, interrupt masks, and debug scratch. Unauthorized access breaks privilege isolation and debug security.

asset_candidates.name: Debug control registers
asset_candidates.description: Registers that activate the debug module, reset harts, select harts, request halt/resume, and configure system bus access (SBA).

asset_candidates.name: JTAG key
asset_candidates.description: 32-bit secret key required for debug authentication. Mismatch blocks DMI operations.

asset_candidates.name: Boot ROM contents
asset_candidates.description: ROM stores initial boot code and may contain keys (pkey_loc_o). Exposure or modification undermines secure boot and key storage.

asset_candidates.name: AES peripheral registers
asset_candidates.description: Crypto accelerator registers holding plaintext, keys, or intermediate cipher state.

asset_candidates.name: Debug mode state
asset_candidates.description: Indicates the hart is in debug mode, granting access to debug-only CSRs and modifying privilege checks.

asset_candidates.name: mtvec lock
asset_candidates.description: Sticky bit that may lock the machine trap-vector base address after initial load, preventing redirection.

subject_candidates.name: External JTAG debugger
subject_candidates.description: Path from JTAG TAP pins (TCK, TMS, TDI, TDO) through DMI protocol to the debug module, capable of issuing DMI transactions.

subject_candidates.name: CPU user-mode request
subject_candidates.description: User-mode software initiating CSR access via standard instruction path (csr_addr_i, csr_wdata_i).

subject_candidates.name: CPU machine-mode request
subject_candidates.description: Machine-mode software with highest CPU privilege, still subject to debug locks and mtvec lock.

subject_candidates.name: Debug System Bus Access master
subject_candidates.description: AXI master inside the debug module that performs memory/peripheral reads and writes on behalf of the debugger, potentially bypassing PMP checks.

subject_candidates.name: Test mode
subject_candidates.description: Special test mode input that overrides internal resets and may allow debug operations without normal authentication/authorization.

operation_candidates.name: read CSR
operation_candidates.description: Read a CSR via CPU instruction or debug abstract command.

operation_candidates.name: write CSR
operation_candidates.description: Write a CSR, potentially altering privilege, address translation, or debug configuration.

operation_candidates.name: set ndmreset
operation_candidates.description: Assert non-debug module reset via DMI write to dmcontrol, resetting the hart.

operation_candidates.name: set dmactive
operation_candidates.description: Activate the debug module, enabling DMI access to hart control and CSRs.

operation_candidates.name: halt hart
operation_candidates.description: Request halt of selected hart via haltreq.

operation_candidates.name: resume hart
operation_candidates.description: Request resume of selected hart via resumereq.

operation_candidates.name: SBA memory read/write
operation_candidates.description: Use system bus access to read or write memory/peripheral space, configured through sbaddress, sbbdata, and sbaccess.

operation_candidates.name: bypass authentication
operation_candidates.description: Skip jtag_key check via test mode or other bypass mechanism.

operation_candidates.name: expose debug scratch
operation_candidates.description: Read dscratch0/1 registers via debug access, potentially leaking sensitive debugger state.

guard_candidates.name: CSR privilege check
guard_candidates.description: Internal logic that determines whether a CSR access is allowed based on current privilege level and csr address. Denied accesses raise read_access_exception.

guard_candidates.name: JTAG key authentication
guard_candidates.description: Compares jtag_key input against a stored expected value; DMI operations are only allowed when pass_chk is asserted.

guard_candidates.name: dmactive gate
guard_candidates.description: The debug module is operational only when dmactive is set; otherwise DMI transactions may be ignored.

guard_candidates.name: ndmreset hold-off
guard_candidates.description: When ndmreset is asserted (low), the hart is held in reset, preventing debug operations.

guard_candidates.name: debug mode check
guard_candidates.description: Certain registers (e.g., dpc, dscratch) are only accessible when debug_mode is active.

guard_candidates.name: mtvec lock
guard_candidates.description: Once set, this sticky bit prevents further writes to mtvec, protecting the trap vector base.

guard_candidates.name: DMI address decode
guard_candidates.description: DMI address field selects which debug register is accessed; illegal addresses may be ignored.

guard_candidates.name: Test mode reset bypass
guard_candidates.description: When testmode_i is high, internal dmi_rst_ni may be overridden, preventing normal reset of security locks and guards.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: debug_mode_q lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Set on debug entry (halt), cleared by dret instruction, reset to 0.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: dmactive_o lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Set by DMI write to dmcontrol, cleared by reset (dmi_rst_ni or ndmreset).

state_lifecycle_facts.name: ndmreset lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Asserted by DMI write to dmcontrol; deasserted when dmactive is cleared or after reset.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: mtvec_rst_load_q lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Sticky bit set after reset and initial mtvec load; might not be cleared until next reset.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: JTAG key check lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.description: pass_chk is evaluated upon DMI access attempt; may be latched until next scan.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: DMI FSM states
state_lifecycle_facts.description: JTAG TAP FSM (test_logic_reset, shift_dr, update_dr, capture_dr) controls when DMI requests are issued and data is captured.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: SBA transaction lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.description: sbaddress/sbdata are written via DMI; a write to sbdata triggers a bus transaction; sbbusy indicates ongoing, sberror_valid indicates error.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: Halt/resume handshake
state_lifecycle_facts.description: haltreq/resumereq outputs set by DMI; halted/resumeack inputs indicate completion.

path_candidates.name: Debug CSR access via DMI
path_candidates.description: JTAG->DMI->Debug Module can issue abstract commands or directly access CSRs through the core interface. Without authentication, allows reading/writing any CSR regardless of privilege level.

path_candidates.name: SBA memory/peripheral access
path_candidates.description: Debugger sets up SBA registers via DMI, then the internal AXI master performs reads/writes on the system bus. This can bypass PMP and access boot ROM, AES registers, etc.

path_candidates.name: Test mode debug activation
path_candidates.description: testmode_i high overrides resets, potentially enabling debug operations without proper key authentication or dmactive gate.

path_candidates.name: CPU privilege bypass via debug halt
path_candidates.description: External debugger halts the hart, enters debug mode (debug_mode_q=1), and then accesses CSRs that normally require high privilege, circumventing read_access_exception checks.

path_candidates.name: Direct CSR access from CPU
path_candidates.description: Normal CPU instruction path requests CSR operations; privilege check is performed, but a flaw could allow illegal access.

path_candidates.name: AES peripheral register access via AXI
path_candidates.description: Debug SBA or another bus master could read/write AES configuration and data registers if no address-based protection exists, exposing keys or plaintext.

path_candidates.name: ROM2 read access via SBA
path_candidates.description: Boot ROM contents, potentially including secret keys, can be read through debug system bus access if no locking mechanism blocks the request.

summary: Identified protected assets including CSR registers, debug control registers, JTAG key, boot ROM, AES crypto registers, debug mode, and mtvec lock. Subjects include external JTAG debugger, CPU privilege modes, debug SBA master, and test mode. Operations encompass CSR read/write, debug control (ndmreset, dmactive, halt/resume), SBA memory access, and authentication bypass. Guards include CSR privilege checks, JTAG key authentication, dmactive/ndmreset gates, debug mode checks, mtvec lock, DMI address decode, and test mode reset bypass. State lifecycle facts cover debug mode, dmactive, ndmreset, mtvec lock, JTAG key checking, DMI FSM, and SBA transactions. Path candidates highlight debug pathways that could circumvent protections: DMI to CSR, SBA to memory/peripherals, test mode activation, debug halt privilege escalation, and direct AXI access to crypto/boot ROM.
